PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER KOPCZYNSKI, JAN A. AS TO RESPONDENT RAM, MICHAEL F. OLSON, KARL CEREGHINO, JEFFREY B. JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JAN KOPCZYNSKI'S MOTION FOR ORDER CONFIRMING PARTIAL FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. JAN KOPCZYNSKI'S "MOTION FOR ORDER CONFIRMING PARTIAL FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT" IS DENIED. "THE ONE FINAL JUDGMENT RULE APPLIES TO JUDGMENTS CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARDS." (KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC. V. SUP. CT. (2017) 13 CAL.APP.5TH 1125, 1139 (CONSTRUING CCP 1283.4).) EVEN WERE THAT NOT SO, I WOULD EXERCISE INHERENT POWERS TO ORDER BUSINESS BEFORE THE COURT TO REACH THE SAME RESULT. "[J]UMPING BACK AND FORTH BETWEEN THE ARBITRATION FORUM AND THE COURTHOUSE" TO CONFIRM "PARTIAL FINAL" ARBITRATION AWARDS IS NOT SENSICAL. (SEE KAISER, 13 CAL.APP.5TH AT 1145-46.) THE PARTIES' BRIEFS DISPUTE THE ARBITRATION'S STATUS, BUT THE FACT REMAINS THAT THE AWARD SAYS "PARTIAL" ON ITS FACE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Confirming Partial Final Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JAN KOPCZYNSKI's Motion For Order Confirming Partial Final Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment.

Jan Kopczynski's "motion for order confirming partial final arbitration award and for entry of judgment" is denied. "The one final judgment rule applies to judgments confirming arbitration awards." (Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1125, 1139 (construing CCP 1283.4).) Even were that not so, I would exercise inherent powers to order business before the court to reach the same result. "[J]umping back and forth between the arbitration forum and the courthouse" to confirm "partial final" arbitration awards is not sensical. (See Kaiser, 13 Cal.App.5th at 1145-46.) The parties' briefs dispute the arbitration's status, but the fact remains that the award says "partial" on its face.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
